item.title: Yoshi's Page-Turning Switch 2 Adventure Debuts In "Top 10 Best-Selling Games" For May 2026 (US)
item.description:
Egg-cellent.

Apart from strong Switch 2 hardware sales last month, multiple first-party Nintendo titles have also featured in the latest US game charts.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Switch 2's First Year Hardware Sales For The US Have Been Revealed
item.description:
A promising start.

Last month, Nintendo's end-of-year financial data revealed the Switch 2 was just shy of 20 million unit sales globally.

As the new hybrid device enters its second year, Circana has now revealed the system's first-year sales in the US. In its first twelve months in this market, the Switch 2 has apparently sold 5.9 million units.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Nintendo Refuses To Answer Whether DS Or 3DS Games Are Coming To Switch 2
item.description:
Obviously.

Y'know, if we were present at some of these Shareholder Q&A sessions at Nintendo, there are so many interesting questions we'd ask. So. Many.

Yet according to OatmealDome on social media (via @NStyles), one particular shareholder decided to ask a question that, realistically, they must have known wouldn't be answered in a satisfactory way.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Guide: 84 Switch 1 & 2 Games You Should Check Out In Nintendo's eShop Summer Sale (US)
item.description:
Summer lovin', happened so Fast RMX.

Summer has kicked off, which means it's time for the obligatory seasonal eShop sale from Nintendo. What better way to appreciate the hot weather than by staying indoors to play games, eh?

You've got until 11:59pm on 8th July 2026 to take advantage of these latest discounts, which actually isn't a great deal of time – you'd better get cracking.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Nintendo Legend Takashi Tezuka Is Retiring From His Role In June
item.description:
Update: He will continue on as a Production Producer.

:

Nintendo has announced that legendary developer Takashi Tezuka will be retiring from his role as Executive Officer.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Nintendo Music Update Adds Animal Crossing: New Horizons - Happy Home Paradise Songs
item.description:
Lottie returns.

Nintendo Music has been receiving updates on a weekly basis and today it's added 25 tracks from the Animal Crossing: New Horizons - Happy Home Paradise soundtrack.

This paid DLC originally arriving in the Switch version fo the game in 2021 and the album has a runtime of 43 minutes. Of course, to listen to this on Nintendo Music, you'll need to have a mobile device or access to a browser, as well as an active Switch Online subscription.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Tomodachi Life: Living The Dream Updated To Version 1.0.3, Here Are The Full Patch Notes
item.description:
Patching island life.

Nintendo has today released a new update for Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream on the Switch, bumping the game up to Version 1.0.3.

This patch resolves a bunch of bugs in the game, and also makes a change to the local play mode, where Mii and other creations can't be sent this way if the island or player name features numbers or text subject to display restrictions.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: PSA: Deltarune Chapter 5 Is Now Available For Switch 1 & 2, Crash Issue Identified
item.description:
Update: A hotfix has been submitted to consoles.

:

Following the Nintendo Direct announcement earlier this month, here's your reminder that Deltarune Chapter 5 is now officially available for Switch 1 & 2.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Super Mario Bros. Wonder "Elephant Mario" Statue Officially Revealed, Here's A First Look
item.description:
Wonderful!

First 4 Figures is known for releasing all sorts of Nintendo-themed figures, and the next one on the way is "Elephant Mario" from Super Mario Bros. Wonder.

It's now teased the "standard edition" of this upcoming figure in its latest showcase, and it will be "coming soon". No price has been revealed just yet, but if you sign up to the First 4 Figures email, you'll be able to get $10 off when pre-orders open on 2nd July 2026. An "exclusive edition" will also be offered.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Video: Dragon's Dogma 2: Dark Arisen Expansion New Gameplay Overview
item.description:
"We plan to add three major elements".

Dragon's Dogma 2: Dark Arisen was one other game recently revealed for the Switch 2, and during the latest Capcom Spotlight this week, fans have now been given another look at the upcoming expansion content, arriving on 9th October 2026.

According to the game's director Kento Kinoshita, this expansion is packed with "enhanced gameplay, uncharted adventures, and enticing value".

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Monster Hunter Stories 3 'Rudy' Side-Story DLC Announced, Out Now Alongside Free Update
item.description:
Exciting news, hunters.

To kick off the latest Capcom Spotlight this week, Capcom announced a new Monster Hunter Stories 3 paid DLC side-story focused on Rudy.

It's all about the young Felyne and explores the origin of the "Royal Felyne", which dates back to the original title Monster Hunter Stories. Here's a bit from the game's director Ryozo Tsujimoto about this:

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Capcom Shares "Exclusive First Peek" Of Onimusha: Way Of The Sword Battle Gameplay
item.description:
Arriving on Switch 2 this September.

Earlier this month, Capcom announced its swordplay action game Onimusha: Way of the Sword would be making its way across to the Switch 2 on 25th September 2026.

Fans of the series have now got another update from the game's director Nihei Satoru in the latest Capcom Spotlight, with an "exclusive first look" at a new Genma known as Dohatsu-ten.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Resident Evil Requiem's Latest Update Is Now Live, Here Are The Full Patch Notes
item.description:
Saving Grace.

Resident Evil Requiem scored a new 'Leon Must Die Forever' minigame mode a few months back, and today, Capcom has released a patch to make sure the bonus is running as well as it should.

The latest update brings things up to ver. 1.3.1, and while some minor tweaks have been made to the game as a whole, the main focus is on the new minigame, where the difficulty has been reduced in its early stages and select skills have been enhanced.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Mini Review: Sonic Frontiers - Definitive Edition (Switch 2) - Fans Deserve Better Than This
item.description:
Blue blur, indeed.

Publishers these days have pretty questionable ideas of what constitutes ‘definitive’, but this really takes the biscuit. After several leaks, Sonic Frontiers - Definitive Edition is out now on Switch 2, but what it actually offers doesn’t justify its name. You’ve got the base game, the ‘Final Horizon’ expansion, and a bunch of other bits and bobs – basically everything that was already provided for free on other platforms. Oh, and there’s no Upgrade Pack. Great start.

Let’s kick off with the positive: this absolutely looks and performs better than the Switch 1 version. So that means it looks pretty great, right? No. Frontiers on Switch 2 is still plagued by atrocious pop-in, including the foliage on the ground, larger trees dotted around the environment, and even entire platforms necessary for completing puzzles. It’s not as bad as Switch 1, but it’s still bad. Capcom, Square Enix, and others have demonstrated just how great games can look this platform, but Sega has gone in the opposite direction; Frontiers on Switch 2 often looks terrible.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Guide: How Star Fox amiibo Unlocks Work On Switch 2
item.description:
Bonus profile customisation options await.

You can use Star Fox amiibo on Switch 2’s Star Fox remake, with various unlocks up for grabs.

There are 12 bonus battle banner elements - three backgrounds, and nine emblems in total - available by using all three compatible amiibo.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Japanese Charts: Combined Console Sales Put The Adventures Of Elliot On Top
item.description:
But Tomodachi Life is the sole best-seller.

Famitsu has provided our latest look at the boxed charts in Japan, and it's The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales that comes out the big winner this week.

Yes, Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream is once again on top with 34,967 units sold, but if we combine the Switch 2 and PS5 sales for Elliot, Square Enix's new release totals 38,517. Pretty good start!

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Feature: My Parents Bought An N64 To Stay Sane With A Nightmare Newborn (Me)
item.description:
"We didn't play it for recreation, we played it as therapy".

The N64 is 30 years old! This week, we're running a series of articles celebrating the 64-bit machine, its industry-shaping software, and its effect on a generation of gamers.

Today, Jim tackles a tough couple of interviewees...

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Nintendo Download: 25th June (North America)
item.description:
Deathbulge! Namco Legendary Mountains! R-Type Tactics!

The latest Nintendo Download update for North America has arrived, and it's bringing new games galore to the eShop. As always, be sure to drop a vote in our poll and comment down below with your potential picks for the week. Enjoy!

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Review: The Drifter - Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Switch 2) - Rivals The Greats Of The Sierra & LucasArts Adventure Era
item.description:
Catch my drift?

Maybe the Golden Age of graphic adventures was not in fact the LucasArts and Sierra era, with its Monkey Islands and Gabriel Knights back around 1990. The Golden Age might just be right now, with a quiet resurgence of brilliant games growing throughout the last decade. This latest arrival on Switch 2 (also available for Switch 1) comes from Australian indie outfit Powerhoof, and it holds its own alongside some of the genre’s best.

The Drifter’s chunky pixels might take you back to the '90s, and its counter-culture antihero and grit might even spark memories of LucasArts’ Full Throttle, but this is not such a sanitised romp. This is not Steven-Spielberg-presents The Dig. The Drifter is sweary, violent and grotesque.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Guide: Star Fox Routes Walkthrough And Alternate Routes Map For Every Stage
item.description:
Plan-et your way ahead.

Star Fox has multiple alternate routes to reach the final planet of Venom, with almost every stage offering regular and secret exits.

Each alternate route has a special, hidden requirement — such as saving or defeating specific characters in battle, completing objectives or flying through warp gates — that allow you to make your own way to the end of the game and face Andross.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Patapon-Inspired Rhythm Game Ratatan Is Delayed Until October
item.description:
"We sincerely apologize for any inconvenience".

Game Source Entertainment and Ratata Arts have announced that the rhythm title Ratatan will be delayed from its initial July release. It's now scheduled to launch on 15th October 2026.

It sounds like development is pretty much done at this point, but work is still ongoing to ensure nasty bugs are squashed that that the Switch 2 title is as stable as possible. Much of this is based on feedback provided from the Steam Early Access release.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Nintendo Announces The Construction Of A New ¥121 Billion R&D Center In Japan
item.description:
Completion is planned for March 2029.

Nintendo has announced it is constructing a new R&D base in Kyoto, Japan, officially called the 'Technology Development Center'.

The company states that the new building "will serve as a new research and development base for Nintendo’s software and hardware", with completion currently planned for March 2029. It had previously acquired the land required for the new building from the City of Kyoto in 2022 following an official proposal process that commenced in 2021.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Another 'Nintendo Switch 2 Edition' Will Deliver More Demon Slaying Action This October
item.description:
An upgrade pack has been confirmed for Switch owners.

Following a release on the original Switch in August last year, Aniplex and developer CyberConnect2 have now finally confirmed a Switch 2 Edition of Demon Slayer -Kimetsu no Yaiba- The Hinokami Chronicles 2.

According to the Weekly Jump reveal, it's locked in for 29th October 2026 in Japan, with a physical edition also confirmed. An upgrade pack has been announced as well, but there are currently no details about the pricing of this. When we get more information about the local release, we'll provide an update.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Square Enix Is Apparently Open To A Nintendo Version Of Final Fantasy XV
item.description:
"It is under consideration".

Square Enix has released multiple Final Fantasy games on Nintendo's hybrid platforms in recent years, so what else could be on the horizon? At the company's most recent shareholders meeting, there was reportedly a question about the possibility of Final Fantasy XV getting a Switch release.

According to a rough translation, Square Enix has acknowledged the idea, claiming a Switch version would not be "completely impossible" from a technical standpoint, so it's apparently "under consideration".

Read the full article on nintendolife.com

item.title: Tetris 99's New Crossover Event Celebrates The Return Of Star Fox, Unlock A Special Theme
item.description:
New Switch icons have also been released.

Star Fox is out today on the Switch 2, and to celebrate Fox McCloud's return, Nintendo has announced a new event for its additive online battle royale title, Tetris 99.

Read the full article on nintendolife.com
